children.text: the effectiveness of such programs in aiding workers, firms, and communities to adjust to changed economic conditions resulting from changes in the patterns of international trade; and
the coordination of the administration of such programs and other Government programs which provide unemployment compensation and relief to depressed areas.

children.heading: Assistance from Labor and Commerce Departments
children.label: b Assistance from Labor and Commerce Departments
children.text: In carrying out his responsibilities under this section, the Comptroller General shall, to the extent practical, avail himself of the assistance of the Departments of Labor and Commerce. The Secretaries of Labor and Commerce shall make available to the Comptroller General any assistance necessary for an effective evaluation of the adjustment assistance programs established under this subchapter.
